fields.name: Błonia Park
fields.description: 48-hectare open meadow at the edge of the city used by locals for runs, cycling, and kite flying. Free, crowd-free on weekdays, and one of the few green spaces in Kraków that doesn't feel like a park.

fields.name: Czartoryski Museum
fields.description: Poland's oldest public museum, home to Leonardo da Vinci's Lady with an Ermine. The building is 14th-century; the collection ranges from ancient Egyptian artefacts to Renaissance paintings.

fields.name: Kazimierz District
fields.description: Kraków's former Jewish quarter, now full of independent cafes, jazz clubs, and second-hand bookshops. Best explored slowly in the evening when the lanterns come on.

fields.name: Kraków Zoo
fields.description: Walk-through enclosures set inside Las Wolski forest. Elephants, giraffes, and a petting paddock for young children. The forest setting makes it feel less like a zoo and more like a nature walk.

fields.name: Oskar Schindler's Factory
fields.description: Award-winning museum documenting Kraków under Nazi occupation, set inside the actual enamel factory. Unsettling and essential — budget at least two hours.

fields.name: Polish Aviation Museum
fields.description: Over 200 aircraft in an open-air display: MiG jets, WWII bombers, and Cold War-era fighters. The outdoor collection is free to walk around; the main hall requires a ticket.

fields.name: Rynek Główny
fields.description: One of Europe's largest medieval market squares. Beautiful at any hour, best at dusk when the Cloth Hall is lit and the crowds thin. Street musicians most evenings in summer.

fields.name: Vistula Cycling Route
fields.description: Flat riverside path from Wawel Hill to Tyniec Abbey and back — about 30 km round trip. Bike rentals available near the riverbank. Views of the city from the opposite bank are the highlight.

fields.name: Wawel Dragon Den
fields.description: The legendary dragon's lair under Wawel Hill. Short cave walk with a fire-breathing steel dragon at the exit. Children under ten will tell the story for years.

fields.name: Wawel Royal Castle Gardens
fields.description: Hillside gardens surrounding Wawel Castle with sweeping views over the Vistula. Quiet on weekday mornings and genuinely romantic at golden hour. Free to enter.

fields.name: Wieliczka Salt Mine
fields.description: UNESCO World Heritage site 135 metres underground: hand-carved salt chapels, brine lakes, and chandeliers made of salt crystals. Book ahead — it sells out most weekends.

fields.name: Zakrzówek Reservoir
fields.description: Former limestone quarry now a turquoise swimming lake popular with local divers and cliff jumpers in summer. Ice skating when it freezes in winter. Not a tourist spot — entirely local.
